Title: Story 10.1: Design System Implementation
Description: As a developer, I want a styleguide showcasing design tokens and components, so that I can ensure consistency and provide a reference for the application. Create a styleguide page showcasing all design tokens, components, and patterns used in the application for consistency and developer reference. Where: New route: /styleguide. What to show: Color palette (Primary, Secondary, Accent, Neutral with hex codes); Typography scale (H1-H6, Body, Caption, Code with font sizes and weights); Spacing tokens (Base unit, scale 4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64px); Component library (Button, Card, Badge, Input etc. with variants); Icon set with names and usage; Border radius and shadow examples; Animation examples (hover, transitions). Component: app/styleguide/page.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Styleguide page accessible at /styleguide route; Shows all colors with hex codes and usage guidelines; Typography scale displayed with actual font sizes; Component showcase with live interactive examples; Icon library with searchable names; Spacing, radius, shadow tokens displayed visually

Title: Story 01.0: Research i3 styleguide
Description: Research i3 styleguide from https://ideation.innovate-bosch.com/. Document and present to team: Color / Typography / Layout / Basic UI elements / Key takeaways
AcceptanceCriteria: Document and present to team findings; From i3 website (https://ideation.innovate-bosch.com/); Cover Color / Typography / Layout / Basic UI elements; Provide Key takeaways

Title: Story 01.3: Update Application Branding to Innovation StarterKit
Description: As a product owner, I want to update all branding references, so that I can establish consistent identity for the Innovation StarterKit. Update all branding references from 'Innovation StarterKit' to the new application name throughout the codebase for consistent identity. Where: Throughout the application. What to update: App name in browser tab title and metadata; Header branding/logo text; Welcome messages and onboarding content; PDF export footer text ('Assisted by [AppName]'); README and documentation references; Package.json name and description; Any hardcoded 'Innovation StarterKit' strings in UI. Component: Global find/replace across codebase, update app/layout.tsx metadata, README.md
AcceptanceCriteria: Browser tab title shows updated application name; Header branding displays new name consistently; Welcome modal and onboarding use new application name; PDF export footer reflects updated branding; README.md and package.json updated with new name/description; No legacy 'Innovation StarterKit' references remain in user-facing UI

Title: Story 09.1: Professional Pitch Deck Design
Description: As an innovator, I want a beautifully designed pitch deck with professional slides, so that I can effectively pitch my idea to stakeholders
AcceptanceCriteria: Modern slide templates with professional layouts; Consistent typography and color scheme; Visual hierarchy with proper spacing; Icon and diagram support; Slide transition animations; Mobile-responsive slide viewer; Dark/light mode support

Title: Story 09.2: Enhanced PDF Export Formatting
Tags: Critical; Dev; Export; M1; PDF; Prototype
Description: As an innovator, I want PDF export with formatting options for cover page and table of contents, so that I can create investor-ready pitch decks. Add professional formatting options for PDF export, including cover page, table of contents, and branding for investor-ready pitch decks. Where: Pitch page export button opens formatting options dialog. What to show: Export button with gear icon for options; Dialog with formatting checkboxes (Cover page, Table of Contents, Slide Notes, Compliance Footer); Preview thumbnail showing selected format; 'Assisted by Innovation-StarterKit' footer option. Component: Enhance lib/export-pdf.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Export button on Pitch page opens formatting dialog; Dialog shows 4 checkbox options for formatting elements; Preview thumbnail updates based on selections; PDF exports with selected formatting applied; Footer includes 'Assisted by Innovation-StarterKit' when enabled

Title: Story 10.4: Responsive Design Optimization
Description: As a mobile user, I want the application optimized for my device, so that I can access and use all features on the go. Optimize the application for mobile devices with responsive navigation, touch-friendly targets, and adaptive layouts. Where: All pages on mobile (<768px). What to optimize: Hamburger menu for navigation (replaces desktop sidebar); Bottom navigation bar for quick phase switching; All tap targets minimum 44x44px; Adaptive typography (smaller base font on mobile); Stack cards vertically (no side-by-side on mobile); Responsive charts (simplified view on mobile); Chat panel becomes full screen on mobile. Component: Update components/wizard/phase-layout.tsx, components/ui/navigation.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Mobile shows hamburger menu in header; Bottom nav bar with 5 phase icons for quick switching; All buttons and tappable areas are minimum 44x44px; Cards stack vertically (no horizontal scrolling); Charts switch to simplified mobile view; Chat panel expands to full width on mobile; No horizontal scroll on any page

Title: Story 01.2: Enhanced Step Navigator
Description: As an innovator, I want visual step indicators showing my progress, so that I can navigate the innovation journey easily. Add visual step indicators showing user's progress through the 5-phase innovation journey, with tooltips showing phase details and ability to jump to previous steps. Where: Top of each phase page (Challenge, Market, Ideation, Investment, Pitch). What to show: 5 horizontal steps with icons (Challenge -> Market -> Ideation -> Investment -> Pitch); Current step highlighted, completed steps with checkmarks, future steps grayed out; Hover tooltip showing step name and brief description; Skip-to dropdown for returning users to jump between completed phases; Progress percentage indicator. Component: Enhance existing components/wizard/progress-tracker.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: 5-step navigator appears at top of all phase pages; Completed steps show green checkmark icon; Current step highlighted with accent color; Hover shows tooltip with phase name and description; Dropdown allows jumping to any completed phase; Progress bar shows overall completion percentage

Title: Story 01.3: Campaign Context Integration
Description: As an innovator responding to a campaign, I want campaign details pre-loaded in my idea, so that I don't have to re-enter information
AcceptanceCriteria: Campaign name and description auto-populated; Campaign deadline displayed in form; Campaign tags/categories pre-selected; Editable fields (user can modify auto-filled content)

Title: Story 01.1: Real-time Idea Validation
Description: As an innovator, I want real-time validation feedback on my form inputs, so that I can correct errors immediately and understand completion status. Add visual feedback to form inputs showing character limits, validation status, and completion progress for better user experience during idea submission. Where: Challenge page form fields (problem description, target audience, current solutions). What to show: Character counter below each textarea (e.g., '50/2000'); Green checkmark icon when field is valid and complete; Red border and error message when validation fails; Progress indicator showing form completion %; Real-time validation as user types (not just on blur/submit). Component: Enhance components/ui/input.tsx and components/ui/textarea.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Character counter appears below textareas (e.g., '150/2000 chars'); Green checkmark shows when field meets minimum requirements; Red outline and error message for invalid input; Overall form progress bar shows completion percentage; Validation happens in real-time as user types

Title: Story 03.1: Image Upload for Ideas
Description: As an innovator, I want to attach images to my ideas, so that I can visually document my concepts and inspirations. Add drag-and-drop image upload to ideation page, allowing users to attach visuals to their ideas. Where: Ideation page, below idea description in IdeaDetailView. What to show: Drop zone with dashed border and upload icon; Drag overlay appears when dragging files over zone; Image preview carousel after upload (horizontal scroll); Remove button (X) on each image; File size error if >5MB; Format error if not JPG/PNG/WebP; Max 5 images limit message. Component: Create components/media/image-upload.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Drop zone appears in idea detail view; Drag and drop or click to upload images; Preview carousel shows uploaded images horizontally; X button on each image to remove; Validation: Max 5 images, 5MB each, JPG/PNG/WebP only; Error messages for invalid files; No backend storage - local preview only for M1

Title: Story 02.1: AI Idea Scoring
Description: As an innovator, I want AI-generated scores with visual indicators for my idea, so that I can understand its strengths and areas for improvement. Display AI-generated idea scores with visual indicators, color-coding, and improvement suggestions for each dimension. Where: IdeaDetailView right panel (below idea details). What to show: 4 score cards (Novelty, Feasibility, Impact, Clarity) 1-10 scale; Color-coded indicators (Green 8-10, Yellow 5-7, Red 1-4); Progress bar or circular indicator for each score; Improvement suggestions for low-scoring areas; Comparison to previous ideas (if available); Overall score with emoji or badge. Component: Create components/ideation/scoring-cards.tsx, integrate into idea-detail-view.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: 4 scoring cards appear in idea detail view; Each card shows dimension name, score (1-10), and color-coded indicator; Green for 8-10, Yellow for 5-7, Red for 1-4; Low scores show improvement suggestions below; Overall score badge at top with emoji; Comparison to other ideas shows relative ranking

Title: Story 02.2: AI Co-Innovator Persona
Description: As an innovator, I want an AI assistant persona with contextual help, so that I can be guided through the innovation process with proactive suggestions. Add an AI assistant persona with avatar, contextual help tips, and proactive suggestions to guide users through the innovation process. Where: Chat panel header, tooltips throughout forms. What to add: AI avatar in chat header with friendly name ('InnovateBot'); Contextual help tooltips on form fields (lightbulb icon); Proactive suggestions ('Have you considered X?') as chips above input; Toggle in settings to enable/disable AI assistance; Greeting message explaining AI's role. Component: Enhance components/chat/phase-chat.tsx, add components/chat/ai-avatar.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Chat header shows AI avatar with name 'Innovation Consultant'; Help tooltip (lightbulb) appears next to form field labels; Suggestion chips show proactive tips above input; Settings toggle: 'AI Assistance: On/Off'; Opening greeting explains AI capabilities; Avatar animates when AI is 'thinking'

Title: Story 11.1: Bosch Innovation Database - Similar Ideas Detection
Tags: Bosch; Database; Deduplication; M1; Mock; Prototype; Similarity; UX
Description: As an innovator, I want to see similar existing innovations from Bosch database, so that I can identify duplicates before development and avoid redundant work. Show similar existing innovations from Bosch database when user submits an idea, helping identify duplicates before development. Where: In AI chat message after idea scoring, and as collapsible card in IdeaDetailView right panel. What to show: 2-3 similar innovations with similarity % badge; Each item: ID (INV-2024-012), title, 1-line summary, stage badge; Color-coded (Red >80%, Yellow 60-80%, Green <60%); Warning icon for high similarity; Click to expand full details. Mock examples: 'AI-Powered Predictive Maintenance' (89%), 'IoT Sensor Network' (67%), 'Digital Twin' (45%). Component: Create components/innovation/similar-ideas-card.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Similar ideas appear in chat after scoring; Dedicated card in IdeaDetailView shows similar ideas; Each idea shows: ID, title, summary, stage, similarity %; Color-coded badges with warning for >80%; Expandable card or modal for full details; Uses static mock data (no API)

Title: Story 11.2: Bosch Innovation Database - Innovation Lifecycle Tracker
Tags: Bosch; Database; Funding; M1; Mock; Pipeline; Prototype; Stages
Description: As an innovator, I want to see my innovation's lifecycle stage with required actions, so that I can understand what steps to take to move forward. Display the innovation application's lifecycle stage with visual progress and required actions to move forward. Where: IdeaDetailView right panel, below idea details. What to show: 9-stage pipeline with current stage highlighted; Progress: 'Stage 3 of 9: Evaluation'; Status badge color (New gray, In Progress blue, Complete green); Required actions: 'Submit budget breakdown by DD/MM'; Activity timeline with last 3-5 status changes and dates. Stages: Draft -> Submitted -> Screening -> Evaluation -> Committee Review -> Approved -> In Progress -> Completed -> Closed. Component: Create components/innovation/lifecycle-tracker.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Lifecycle tracker card shows all 9 stages; Current stage highlighted with distinct color; Progress indicator shows 'Stage X of 9'; Next Steps section shows required actions; Activity timeline with 3-5 mock status updates; Clickable stages show requirements in tooltip; Uses mock data (no backend)

Title: Story 11.4: Bosch Innovation Database - Focus Area Insights
Tags: Bosch; Database; Experts; FocusArea; Insights; M1; Mock; Prototype
Description: As an innovator, I want focus area information with expert contacts, so that I can connect with domain experts and understand strategic context. Show focus area information when idea is categorized, helping innovators understand strategic context and find experts. Where: IdeaDetailView right panel, below lifecycle tracker. What to show: Focus area name with priority badge (High/Medium/Low); Brief description (1-2 sentences); Key Experts: 3-5 cards with avatar, name, role, email icon; Recent Innovations: 2-3 successful examples with year; Funding Trend: Sparkline or mini bar chart (4 quarters). Mock example (AI/ML): Priority High, Experts 'Dr. Sarah Chen - AI Lead', Recent 'Neural QC (2024)', Funding: Growing. Component: Create components/innovation/focus-area-insights.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Focus area card shows name, priority badge, description; Experts section with avatar/name/role/contact icon; Recent innovations list with title and year; Funding trend visualization (sparkline or bars); Collapsible to save space; Uses mock data from idea's technologies/industries

Title: Story 10.2: Micro-Interactions and Animations
Description: As a user, I want subtle animations and micro-interactions throughout the UI, so that I can feel a polished and responsive experience. Add subtle animations and micro-interactions throughout the UI for better feedback and polished feel. Where: Throughout the app (buttons, cards, forms, page transitions). What to add: Button hover/active states with scale/color transitions; Loading skeleton for async content (shimmer effect); Success/error toast animations (slide in + fade); Smooth page transitions between wizard steps; Card hover effect (slight elevation + shadow increase); Form field focus ring animation; Progress bar fill animation. Component: Update components/ui/button.tsx, add components/ui/loading-skeleton.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Buttons have smooth hover (scale 1.02) and active (scale 0.98) transitions; Loading skeleton shows shimmer animation during data fetch; Toast messages slide in from right with fade effect; Page transitions have smooth fade between phases; Cards elevate slightly (shadow increase) on hover; Form inputs show animated focus ring; Progress bars animate from 0 to actual value

Title: Story 10.3: Loading States and Empty States
Description: As a user, I want clear loading and empty state feedback, so that I can understand what's happening and what actions to take. Create reusable loading and empty state components for consistent feedback when content is loading or unavailable. Where: Throughout app (idea list, dashboard, any async content area). What to show: Loading (Spinner icon or skeleton screens); Empty (Illustration, helpful message, call-to-action button); Error (Error icon, message explaining issue, retry button). Mock examples: Loading ideas (3 skeleton cards with shimmer); No ideas (Lightbulb icon + 'No ideas yet. Click Generate Ideas to start.'); Error (Alert triangle + 'Failed to load. Please try again.' + Retry button). Component: Create components/ui/loading-skeleton.tsx, components/ui/empty-state.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Loading skeleton shows 3 placeholder cards with shimmer animation; Empty state shows icon + message + CTA button; Error state shows icon + message + retry button; Components accept props for custom message and action; Used consistently across all list views and data displays

Title: Story 10.8: Error Handling and Validation UX
Description: As a user, I want inline error messages with specific guidance, so that I can fix mistakes easily without frustration. Add inline error validation to forms with specific guidance, confirmation dialogs for destructive actions, and toast notifications. Where: All form pages (Challenge, Market, etc.). What to add: Inline error messages below each field; Specific guidance (not just 'Required' -> 'Please describe your challenge (min 50 chars)'); Warning state for risky actions (yellow border + icon); Confirmation dialog before destructive actions (delete, clear, reset); Toast notifications for success/error feedback. Component: Enhance components/ui/form.tsx, components/ui/toast.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria: Inline error messages appear below invalid fields; Error messages are specific and actionable; Warning state shows yellow border with icon for risky actions; Confirmation dialog appears before delete/clear operations; Toast notifications show success/error messages; Form-level error summary at top when submitting with errors

Title: Story 05.1: Analytics Dashboard (Simplified)
Description: As a product owner, I want an analytics dashboard showing innovation metrics, so that I can track ideas, trends, and campaign participation. Create an analytics dashboard showing innovation metrics with charts and mock data. Where: New route: /analytics (accessible from header or settings). What to show: Bar chart (Ideas by category - Manufacturing, Healthcare, Automotive, etc.); Line chart (Ideas submitted over time - last 6 months); Pie chart (Campaign participation distribution); Summary cards (Total ideas, Avg score, In progress, Completed); Export buttons (CSV, PDF). Mock data examples: Manufacturing 45 ideas, Healthcare 32, Automotive 28, Other 15; Trend Jan=8, Feb=12, Mar=15, Apr=18, May=22, Jun=25. Component: Create app/analytics/page.tsx using Recharts
AcceptanceCriteria: Analytics page accessible at /analytics route; Bar chart shows ideas by category with labels; Line chart shows submission trend over 6 months; Pie chart shows campaign distribution with legend; 4 summary cards at top with key metrics; Export to CSV and PDF buttons (mock functionality); All data is mock for M1

Title: Story 10.5: Dark/Light Mode Toggle (Simplified)
Description: As a user, I want to toggle between dark and light modes, so that I can use the application comfortably in different lighting conditions. Add a theme toggle button to switch between dark and light modes with smooth transition animation. Where: Header next to user profile/settings menu. What to show: Sun/Moon icon button for theme toggle; Smooth transition animation when switching (0.3s ease); Theme persists in localStorage; Auto theme detection option (optional for M1); Consistent color contrast in both themes. Component: Create components/theme/theme-toggle.tsx, integrate into header
AcceptanceCriteria: Theme toggle button appears in header; Sun icon in light mode, Moon icon in dark mode; Smooth color transition (0.3s) when switching; Selected theme saved to localStorage; All components work in both light and dark modes; Color contrast meets accessibility standards
